description:
The SSH server service, sshd, is commonly needed. The sshd service can be enabled with the following command: 
$ sudo systemctl enable sshd.service
rationale:
Without protection of the transmitted information, confidentiality, and integrity may be compromised because unprotected communications can be intercepted and either read or altered.

This checklist item applies to both internal and external networks and all types of information system components from which information can be transmitted (e.g., servers, mobile devices, notebook computers, printers, copiers, scanners, etc). Communication paths outside the physical protection of a controlled boundary are exposed to the possibility of interception and modification.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine the current status of the sshd service: 
$ sudo systemctl is-active sshd
If the service is running, it should return the following: 
active
fixtext:
To enable the sshd service run the following command:

$ sudo systemctl enable --now sshd
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 service sshd must be enabled.

stig.srg_requirement: All Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 networked systems must have and implement SSH to protect the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted and received information, as well as information during preparation for transmission.
stig.vuldiscussion:
Without protection of the transmitted information, confidentiality and integrity may be compromised because unprotected communications can be intercepted and either read or altered.

This requirement applies to both internal and external networks and all types of information system components from which information can be transmitted (e.g., servers, mobile devices, notebook computers, printers, copiers, scanners, and facsimile machines). Communication paths outside the physical protection of a controlled boundary are exposed to the possibility of interception and modification.

Protecting the confidentiality and integrity of organizational information can be accomplished by physical means (e.g., employing physical distribution systems) or by logical means (e.g., employing cryptographic techniques). If physical means of protection are employed, then logical means (cryptography) do not have to be employed, and vice versa.
stig.checktext:
Verify that "sshd" is active with the following command:

$ systemctl is-active sshd

active

If the "sshd" service is not active, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
To enable the sshd service run the following command:

$ systemctl enable --now sshd

title: Enable the OpenSSH Service
